title: Doubanjiang Syrian Meat Tagine
description: A funky, crumbly, meal-prep Syrian dish featuring doubanjiang and tempering-chocolate, no onion or garlic in sight. This recipe makes the perfect weeknight dinner or prep-for-the-week meal.
instructions: Combine ground beef, salt, black pepper, and cumin in a large mixing bowl, mix well.
Add doubanjiang and tempering chocolate, mix until just combined.
Heat a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat, add the beef mixture and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the skillet or Dutch oven to the oven and bake at 400°F (200°C) for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
Stir in chopped parsley and cilantro before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
Let cool completely and refrigerate or freeze for meal prep.
Reheat as needed, serving from the refrigerator or freezer directly without further cooking.
To make the dish easier to reheat, consider portioning it into individual containers before refrigerating or freezing.
